scaffolds.q: Sensory receptors convert environmental stimuli into electrical signals.
scaffolds.explain: Receptors convert stimuli (pressure, light, chemicals, heat) into electrical signals that neurons can process. This conversion is essential for ALL sensation. Without transduction, you'd be deaf, blind, and numb. Let that sink in.

scaffolds.q: A graded potential is an electrical change that varies in size with the stimulus strength.
scaffolds.explain: Unlike action potentials (all-or-nothing), graded potentials vary in amplitude. A stronger stimulus produces a larger receptor potential. It's analog encoding before digital transmission.
